channel.title: Posts on Christopher Hannan
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on Christopher Hannan

item.title: ACI - Converting NX-OS to ACI Boot Mode
item.description: Converting to ACI Boot Mode Follow the steps below to convert a Cisco Nexus 9000 Series switch from NX-OS standalone mode to ACI boot mode. 1. Verify which mode the switch is running (ACI or NX-OS) Power on the new switch and connect a console to check if it is running in ACI mode or NX-OS mode. Cisco NX-OS filenames begin with “n9000” while ACI filenames begin with “aci-n9000”: switch# show version 2.
item.content:
Converting to ACI Boot Mode

Follow the steps below to convert a Cisco Nexus 9000 Series switch from NX-OS standalone mode to ACI boot mode.

1. Verify which mode the switch is running (ACI or NX-OS)

Power on the new switch and connect a console to check if it is running in ACI mode or NX-OS mode. Cisco NX-OS filenames begin with “n9000” while ACI filenames begin with “aci-n9000”:

switch# show version

2. Uploading the ACI image

The ACI image can be copied to the switch via SCP from the APIC, an SCP server or directly via USB.

Method 1: Using SCP from the APIC

Configure management connectivity to the APIC

From the APIC CLI, view the ACI Out-of-band information and find a spare IP in the management network to use on the new switch:

apic1# show oob-mgmt

Set the IP address on the mgmt0 interface of the new switch to allow connectivity between this interface and the APIC:

Example:

switch(config)# interface mgmt0

switch(config-if)# ip address <ip-address/length>

Patch the new switch into a spare port on the OOB management switch in the management VLAN. Confirm connectivity from the new switch to the APIC:

switch# ping <apic-ip-address>

Copy the ACI image from the APIC to the switch

Enable SCP services on the switch:

switch(config)# feature scp-server

From the APIC CLI, view and take note of the currently installed leaf/spine switch firmware versions:

apic1# show versions

View the firmware images present in the APIC repository and find the “aci” version of the above switch firmware. Take note of the MD5 checksum:

apic1# show firmware repository detail

Use SCP to copy the firmware image from the APIC repository to the active supervisor module on the switch:

apic1# scp -r /firmware/fwrepos/fwrepo/<aci-image.bin> admin@<switch-ip-address>:<aci-image.bin>

Method 2: Using USB

Copy the image from the USB drive to bootflash

switch# dir usb1:
switch# copy usb1:<aci-image.bin> bootflash:

Note: For dual-supervisor systems, copy the ACI image from the active to the standby supervisor module. Example: switch(config)# copy bootflash:<aci-image.bin> bootflash://sup-standby/

3. Boot the switch to the ACI image

Configure the switch to not boot from Cisco NX-OS:

switch(config)# no boot nxos

Save the configuration:

switch(config)# copy running-config startup-config

Note: You must run the copy running-config startup-config command prior to booting the ACI image. Do not run it after you enter the boot aci command.

Boot the switch with the ACI image:

switch(config)# boot aci bootflash:<aci-image.bin>

Caution: Do not enter the copy running-config startup-config command after the boot aci command. If you do, the switch will go to the loader> prompt.

Verify the integrity of the file by displaying the MD5 checksum and comparing it to that noted in Step 2:

switch(config)# show file bootflash:<aci-image.bin> md5sum

Check the output of the show boot command to see if the bootvar is correctly set:

switch# show boot

Reload the switch:

switch# reload

Log in to the switch as an administrator to verify it is now running in ACI mode. Notice that the fabric discovery process has been started at this point. It may be some minutes before the services start and we are able to access the switch via the console.:

Example:

User Access Verification
(none) login: admin
*****************************************************************************
    Fabric discovery in progress, show commands are not fully functional
    Logout and Login after discovery to continue to use show commands.
*****************************************************************************

item.title: ACI - Basic Commands
item.description: General Overview acidiag fnvread Displays the address and state of switch nodes registered with the fabric. Moquery Usage moquery --help usage: Command line cousin to visore [-h] [-i HOST] [-p PORT] [-d DN] [-c KLASS] [-f FILTER] [-a ATTRS] [-o OUTPUT] [-u USER] [-x [OPTIONS [OPTIONS ...]]] optional arguments: -h, --help show this help message and exit -i HOST, --host HOST Hostname or ip of apic -p PORT, --port PORT REST server port -d DN, --dn DN dn of the mo -c KLASS, --klass KLASS comma seperated class names to query -f FILTER, --filter FILTER property filter to accept/reject mos -a ATTRS, --attrs ATTRS type of attributes to display (config, all) -o OUTPUT, --output OUTPUT Display format (block, table, xml, json) -u USER, --user USER User name -x [OPTIONS [OPTIONS .
item.content:
General Overview

acidiag fnvread Displays the address and state of switch nodes registered with the fabric.

Moquery Usage

moquery --help
usage: Command line cousin to visore [-h] [-i HOST] [-p PORT] [-d DN]
                                     [-c KLASS] [-f FILTER] [-a ATTRS]
                                     [-o OUTPUT] [-u USER]
                                     [-x [OPTIONS [OPTIONS ...]]]

optional arguments:
  -h, --help            show this help message and exit
  -i HOST, --host HOST  Hostname or ip of apic
  -p PORT, --port PORT  REST server port
  -d DN, --dn DN        dn of the mo
  -c KLASS, --klass KLASS
                        comma seperated class names to query
  -f FILTER, --filter FILTER
                        property filter to accept/reject mos
  -a ATTRS, --attrs ATTRS
                        type of attributes to display (config, all)
  -o OUTPUT, --output OUTPUT
                        Display format (block, table, xml, json)
  -u USER, --user USER  User name
  -x [OPTIONS [OPTIONS ...]], --options [OPTIONS [OPTIONS ...]]
                        Extra options to the query

Get help

moquery -h
or
man moquery

Query all tenants

moquery -c fvTenant -o table

Query specific tenant

moquery -d uni/tn-TenantTest123

Using moquery to check/sort active faults (faultInst)
moquery -c faultInst | egrep -e "^descr" | sort | uniq -c
or
moquery -c fvTenant -f 'fv.Tenant.name=="TenantTest123"'
or
moquery -c fvTenant -x 'query-target-filter=eq(fvTenant.name,"TenantTest123")'

Query specific tenant and all children

moquery -d uni/tn-TenantTest123 -x "rsp-subtree=children rsp-subtree-class=fvBD"

Query active faults

moquery -c faultInst | egrep -e "^descr" | sort | uniq -c

Query faults by description

moquery –c faultInst –f fault.Inst.descr==“: Failed to form relation to MO AbsGraph-VEStandAloneFuncProfile …”

Syslog

Generate a custom syslog message:
logit severity <severity> dest-grp <WORD> server <Remote Dest Name> <Syslog message> node <Source node>

Example:
logit severity 1 dest-grp splunk server 10.180.163.54 "THIS IS A TEST"

Local Users

apic1# configure terminal 
apic1(config)# username user5
apic1(config-username)# first-name George
apic1(config-username)# last-name Washington
apic1(config-username)# email gwashington@exampleCorp.com
apic1(config-username)# phone 14085551212
apic1(config-username)# account-status active
apic1(config-username)# domain mySecDomain
apic1(config-username)# clear-pwd-history
apic1(config-username)# expires
apic1(config-username)# expiration 2017-12-31T23:59+08:00
apic1(config-username)# password 
Password: c1$c0123
apic1(config-username)# pwd-lifetime 90
apic1(config-username)# domain mySecDomain
apic1(config-domain)# role tenant-admin
apic1(config-role)# priv-type writePriv
apic1(config-role)# exit
apic1(config-domain)# exit
apic1(config-username)# show username user5
UserName                : user5
First-Name              : George
Last-Name               : Washington
Email                   : gwashington@exampleCorp.com
Acount Status           : active
Password strength check : yes

item.title: VM - Base Build
item.description: Update All Base Packages sudo apt -y update sudo apt -y upgrade sudo apt-get install software-properties-common reboot Install Additional Tools # Misc sudo apt -y install open-vm-tools sudo apt -y install python-pip sudo apt -y install python3-pip sudo apt-get -y install python3-venv sudo apt -y install tree sudo pip install pyopenssl sudo pip3 install 'ansible==2.8.6' sudo pip3 install paramiko #Terminator sudo apt-get -y install terminator #Sublime Text wget -qO - https://download.
item.content:
Update All Base Packages

sudo apt -y update
sudo apt -y upgrade
sudo apt-get install software-properties-common
reboot

Install Additional Tools

# Misc
sudo apt -y install open-vm-tools
sudo apt -y install python-pip
sudo apt -y install python3-pip
sudo apt-get -y install python3-venv
sudo apt -y install tree
sudo pip install pyopenssl
sudo pip3 install 'ansible==2.8.6'
sudo pip3 install paramiko

#Terminator
sudo apt-get -y install terminator

#Sublime Text
wget -qO - https://download.sublimetext.com/sublimehq-pub.gpg | sudo apt-key add -
sudo apt-add-repository "deb https://download.sublimetext.com/ apt/stable/"
sudo apt-get update
sudo apt-get install sublime-text

#Generate SSH Key and add to Git
ssh-keygen -o -t rsa -b 4096 -C <email_address>
cat ~/.ssh/id_rsa.pub
ssh -T <git@git...>
